Nf3 Nc6 3. c3 Nf6 |eco=[[Chess/ECOC|C44]] |parent=[[../|Ponziani opening]] }} == 3...Nf6 · Jaenisch counterattack == '''3...Nf6''', the [[Wikipedia:Carl Jaenisch|Jaenisch counterattack]], is Black's most popular retort to the Ponziani and most serious alternative to [[../3...d5/|3...d5]]. Nf6 targets White's e4 pawn, taking advantage of the fact that White cannot play Nc3 to defend it. White may continue with '''[[/4. d4|4. d4]]'''. This offers Black their choice of central pawns to take (4...Nxe4 or 4...exd4), after which White intends to push their other pawn to gain tempo and recover the pawn. '''4. d3''' to defend e4 is playable, but leaves White's pawn on c3 looking out of place and allows Black to take the centre with 4...d5. No other reply for White seems credible. ==Theory table== {{Chess Opening Theory/Table}} '''1.e4 e5 2.
